skill.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
skill.description: Sets up an `## Agent skills` block in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and `docs/agents/` so the engineering skills know this repo's issue tracker (GitHub or local markdown), triage label vocabulary, and domain doc layout. Run before first use of `to-issues`, `to-prd`, `triage`, `diagnose`, `tdd`, `improve-codebase-architecture`, or `zoom-out` — or if those skills appear to be missing context about the issue tracker, triage labels, or domain docs.
skill.summary: Configures per-repo settings for Matt Pocock's engineering skill suite
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner flagged markdown backtick syntax as shell execution, and documentation URLs as network exfiltration. This skill is a legitimate configuration wizard that explores the repo structure, asks users interactive questions about their issue tracker and triage setup, then writes configuration files to docs/agents/. The skill requires user confirmation before writing, includes good guardrails (never overwrites existing sections, checks for CLAUDE.md vs AGENTS.md), and contains no malicious code or data exfiltration patterns.

medium_findings.title: File System Write Operations
medium_findings.description: Skill writes configuration files to user-specified locations including CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and docs/agents/. While the skill requires user confirmation before writing, the paths are based on user input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill writes files but includes user confirmation steps and guardrails against overwriting existing content

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Shell Command Syntax
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 140 locations. These are markdown documentation files using backtick syntax (` `command` `) for displaying CLI commands like `gh issue create`. This is standard markdown formatting, not actual shell execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Files are pure markdown documentation - backticks are markdown inline code syntax, not shell execution operators

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Hardcoded URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged hardcoded URLs at 2 locations. These are documentation links to the glab CLI tool homepage (https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/cli). Not exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: URLs are legitimate documentation links to the GitLab CLI tool's official homepage

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged 'weak_crypto: weak_cipher' at 8 locations. These files contain only markdown documentation. No cryptographic operations exist.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Source files are pure markdown with no cryptographic operations present

content.user_title: Set up Matt Pocock's engineering skills
content.value_statement: This skill configures your repository with the issue tracker, triage labels, and domain docs settings that Matt Pocock's engineering skills need to work correctly. Run once before using any of the related engineering skills.
actual_capabilities: Detects existing repository configuration by reading git remote, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and docs/agents/
Interactively walks users through three setup decisions: issue tracker type, triage label vocabulary, and domain docs layout
Creates or updates the Agent skills section in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md
Generates configuration files in docs/agents/ for issue tracker, triage labels, and domain docs
Supports GitHub, GitLab, local markdown, and custom issue tracker workflows
Handles both single-context and multi-context repository layouts
limitations: Only configures Matt Pocock's engineering skills, not other skill sets
Does not create GitHub/GitLab issues or credentials - assumes gh/glab CLI is already authenticated
Cannot migrate existing issues between trackers or change triage labels retroactively
Requires user interaction for setup decisions - cannot be run fully automated

use_cases.title: New repository onboarding
use_cases.description: When starting a new project, run this skill once to configure the issue tracker and triage labels. The skill will detect your git remote and suggest the appropriate tracker type.
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting new projects who want to use Matt Pocock's engineering workflow

use_cases.title: Team standards alignment
use_cases.description: Team leads can use this skill to standardize issue tracker conventions across the team. The skill generates consistent configuration files that all team members' AI assistants will use.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering team leads establishing codebase conventions

use_cases.title: Troubleshooting missing context
use_cases.description: If other engineering skills seem to lack context about your issue tracker or triage labels, re-run this skill to regenerate the configuration files and verify settings.
use_cases.target_user: Developers debugging skill behavior or switching issue trackers

prompt_templates.title: Initial setup for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the setup-matt-pocock-skills skill to configure this repository for Matt Pocock's engineering workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup when starting to use the engineering skill suite

prompt_templates.title: Check current configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Run setup-matt-pocock-skills to show me the current configuration and confirm what issue tracker and triage labels are set up.
prompt_templates.scenario: Verifying existing setup without making changes

prompt_templates.title: Switch to GitLab
prompt_templates.prompt: Run setup-matt-pocock-skills and update the issue tracker configuration to use GitLab instead of GitHub.
prompt_templates.scenario: Migrating from GitHub to GitLab issue tracking

prompt_templates.title: Add custom triage labels
prompt_templates.prompt: Run setup-matt-pocock-skills and update the triage labels to match our team convention where we use bug:triage instead of needs-triage.
prompt_templates.scenario: Customizing triage label vocabulary to match team standards

output_examples.input: Run setup-matt-pocock-skills to configure this repository
output_examples.output:
## Setup Complete

I've configured the following for this repository:

**Issue tracker:** GitHub (github.com/mattpocock/skills)
**Triage labels:** Default vocabulary (needs-triage, needs-info, ready-for-agent, ready-for-human, wontfix)
**Domain docs:** Single-context layout

Configuration files written to docs/agents/

The following skills will now read from these files: to-issues, triage, to-prd, diagnose, tdd, improve-codebase-architecture, zoom-out

output_examples.input: What issue tracker is currently configured?
output_examples.output:
Current configuration:
- Issue tracker: GitHub
- Repo: github.com/example/project
- Triage labels: Custom (bug:triage, enhancement:review, question:answered)
- Domain docs: Multi-context layout (CONTEXT-MAP.md detected)

To change any of these, run setup-matt-pocock-skills again.

best_practices: Run this skill once before using any of Matt Pocock's engineering skills for the first time in a new repository
Review the generated configuration files in docs/agents/ and edit them directly if you need to make small adjustments later
Re-run this skill only when switching issue trackers or resetting configuration - otherwise use direct file edits for small changes
anti_patterns: Don't skip running this setup - other engineering skills depend on the configuration files it creates
Don't modify CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md sections manually if you've run this skill - edits may be overwritten on re-run
Don't use local markdown tracking for repositories with multiple contributors who need to access issues

faq.question: Do I need to run this skill every time I start a new project?
faq.answer: Yes, each repository needs its own configuration. The skill detects your git remote to determine the appropriate settings for each project.

faq.question: What happens if I skip running this setup?
faq.answer: Other engineering skills like to-issues, triage, and tdd will not know where to create issues or what triage labels to use, and may not function correctly.

faq.question: Can I use both GitHub and GitLab for the same repository?
faq.answer: No, the configuration picks one issue tracker per repository. If you use both platforms, create separate clones or repositories for each.

faq.question: What if my team uses custom triage labels?
faq.answer: The skill lets you customize the label strings during setup. You can also edit docs/agents/triage-labels.md directly at any time.

faq.question: Does this skill require network access?
faq.answer: It reads your git remote to detect the repository URL, but after initial setup, all configuration is stored locally in the docs/agents/ directory.

faq.question: Can I undo the changes made by this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill only writes to CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and docs/agents/. You can delete these files or revert with git to undo the changes.
